title: Explain Browser
short_answer: Browser is the app block that controls browser actions.
text: In this app, the browser block can open pages, search, click visible text, save page text, and provide browser context for later questions.

title: Explain Open Browser
short_answer: Open Browser starts or focuses a browser session.
text: Use browser open behavior when the assistant needs a browser window available for search, page navigation, clicks, or saved page text.

title: Explain Search
short_answer: Search tells the browser to look up a query.
text: In this app, search should safely handle normal search text instead of assuming every phrase is a URL.

title: Explain Navigation
short_answer: Navigation decides what browser page or search URL to open.
text: Navigation should open real URLs directly and convert non-URLs into search URLs. This prevents bad browser input from crashing the browser flow.

title: Explain Click Text
short_answer: Click Text clicks visible page text by name.
text: Click Text is useful when the user says the text they see on the page. The browser should search visible text and click the best matching element.

title: Explain Visible Text
short_answer: Visible Text is the text currently shown on the browser page.
text: Visible text helps the assistant understand what can be clicked or read on the page without relying on coordinates.

title: Explain Save Text
short_answer: Save Text saves browser page text for later context.
text: When the assistant saves page text, it should write the text to a runtime knowledge/temp file and update the active saved text pointer.

title: Explain Browser Context
short_answer: Browser Context is saved page text used by later questions.
text: Browser context lets the LLM answer questions about a page after the page text has been saved. This avoids relying only on the live browser view.

title: Explain Latest Saved Text
short_answer: Latest Saved Text points to the current browser text context file.
text: The latest saved text pointer tells the app which saved browser text should be used as active context for LLM direct questions.

title: Explain Browser Service
short_answer: Browser Service owns browser actions for the app.
text: Browser Service should be the clean layer that executes browser open, search, click, scroll, and save text actions instead of scattering Playwright logic everywhere.
